description_long: This object is a vintage wooden radio receiver with a rectangular box shape and rounded vertical edges. The front face features a woven speaker grille occupying the upper half, flanked by two large, dark circular knobs on either side, and a horizontal frequency indicator panel below the grille. The wood casing has a warm, light brown finish with darker trim along the edges, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, highlighting the natural wood grain. The back and bottom are not visible, but the overall construction suggests a solid, well-crafted housing typical of radios from the era. The object is likely hollow inside to accommodate electronic components and a speaker.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a woven speaker grille, two large knobs, and a horizontal frequency indicator on the front. The casing is light brown wood with darker trim and a smooth, classic finish.
description_view_0: The side view shows the smooth, light brown wooden surface with rounded vertical edges and no visible controls or features.
description_view_1: The rear or opposite side is a plain, flat wooden panel with visible wood grain and no interactive elements.
description_view_2: This side view mirrors view 0, displaying the same smooth, unadorned wooden surface and rounded edges.
description_view_3: The front view reveals the woven speaker grille, two large dark knobs, and a horizontal frequency indicator panel, all framed by the wooden casing.
